test_cases.title: Successful Login with Valid Credentials
test_cases.description: Test the login functionality with valid username and password to ensure successful authentication and redirection to the Accounts Overview page.
steps: Enter a valid Username in the Username field.
Enter a valid Password in the Password field.
test_cases.expected_results: The user is successfully authenticated, redirected to the 'Accounts Overview' page, a welcome message with the user's first and last name is displayed, and the 'Log Out' link is visible in the left-hand menu.

test_cases.title: Login with Invalid Credentials
test_cases.description: Test the login functionality with invalid username and/or password to ensure authentication is denied and an appropriate error message is displayed.
steps: Enter an invalid Username in the Username field.
Enter an invalid Password in the Password field.
test_cases.expected_results: The user is not authenticated, remains on the login page, and an error message is displayed indicating invalid login credentials.

test_cases.title: Login with Empty Credentials
test_cases.description: Test the login functionality with empty username and password fields to ensure authentication is denied and an appropriate error message is displayed.
test_cases.expected_results: The user is not authenticated, remains on the login page, and an error message is displayed indicating that username and password are required.

test_cases.title: SQL Injection in Username Field
test_cases.description: Test the login functionality for SQL injection vulnerability by entering a SQL statement in the username field.
steps: Enter a SQL injection string (e.g., ' OR '1'='1) in the Username field.
Enter any text in the Password field.
test_cases.expected_results: The user is not authenticated, remains on the login page, and an error message is displayed. The application should not execute the SQL statement.

test_cases.title: SQL Injection in Password Field
test_cases.description: Test the login functionality for SQL injection vulnerability by entering a SQL statement in the password field.
steps: Enter a valid Username in the Username field.
Enter a SQL injection string (e.g., ' OR '1'='1) in the Password field.
test_cases.expected_results: The user is not authenticated, remains on the login page, and an error message is displayed. The application should not execute the SQL statement.

test_cases.title: Login with Long Username and Password
test_cases.description: Test the login functionality with excessively long username and password to ensure the application handles it gracefully.
steps: Enter a long string (e.g., 256 characters) in the Username field.
Enter a long string (e.g., 256 characters) in the Password field.
test_cases.expected_results: The user is not authenticated, remains on the login page, and an error message is displayed indicating invalid login credentials. The application should not crash or behave unexpectedly.

test_cases.title: Boundary Value Analysis for Username Field
test_cases.description: Test the login functionality with boundary values for the username field to ensure it handles minimum and maximum lengths correctly.
steps: Enter a username with the minimum allowed length (e.g., 1 character) in the Username field.
Enter a valid Password in the Password field.
Repeat the steps with a username of maximum allowed length (e.g., 50 characters).
test_cases.expected_results: For both minimum and maximum length usernames, the user should be authenticated if the credentials are valid, otherwise, an error message should be displayed.

test_cases.title: Boundary Value Analysis for Password Field
test_cases.description: Test the login functionality with boundary values for the password field to ensure it handles minimum and maximum lengths correctly.
steps: Enter a valid Username in the Username field.
Enter a password with the minimum allowed length (e.g., 1 character) in the Password field.
Repeat the steps with a password of maximum allowed length (e.g., 50 characters).
test_cases.expected_results: For both minimum and maximum length passwords, the user should be authenticated if the credentials are valid, otherwise, an error message should be displayed.
